title: CAR SEAT PROGRAM - 211info
text: PENDLETON WIC CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seats to low-income families at a reduced cost. Car seat availability is dependent on community donations. 455 SW 13th Street Room 162 Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Low-income households, WIC and headstart Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 966-3354 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 966-3354 Main phone car seat (541) 564-6878 ext 231 April Utecht [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Varies DOCUMENTS: Proof of current Oregon Health Plan, Oregon Trail Card, WIC Voucher (Women, Infants, and Children), or SSI (social security income) LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Morrow and Umatilla Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Services: Child Passenger Safety Seats Other Services Offered At This Location: PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. PENDLETON WIC
